stories.headline: Avalanche trade Samuel Girard, second-round pick to Penguins for Brett Kulak
stories.summary:
The Colorado Avalanche made a significant blueline move Tuesday, shipping defenseman Samuel Girard and a 2028 second-round pick to the Pittsburgh Penguins in exchange for veteran defender Brett Kulak, 10 days before the NHL trade deadline.

Girard, 27, had been the longest-tenured member of Colorado's defense corps, playing 583 regular-season games and 67 playoff games for the Avalanche after arriving in a blockbuster 2017 three-team trade that also netted the pick that became Bowen Byram. He carries a $5 million cap hit and is signed through next season. Kulak, 32, is an unrestricted free agent after this season with a $2.75 million cap hit, providing the Avalanche $2.25 million in immediate cap relief.

Kulak brings more than 600 regular-season games and 98 playoff games of experience, having reached the Stanley Cup Final three times — with Montreal in 2021 and Edmonton in each of the past two seasons. He averaged 23:25 of ice time per game during Edmonton's 2025 playoff run.

Coach Jared Bednar praised Kulak's defensive reliability and skating ability while acknowledging the emotional difficulty of parting with Girard, calling the longtime Avalanche defender 'an unbelievable teammate.' General manager Chris MacFarland said Kulak's ability to play in all situations and his playoff experience were central to the acquisition.

stories.headline: Denver police arrest suspect in two separate attacks on women in January
stories.summary:
Denver police announced Monday the arrest of Chanse Leon Lipscomb, 24, in connection with two separate assaults on women that occurred weeks apart in January in the South Grant Street area of Denver.

The first incident took place Jan. 7 around 5:30 p.m. in the 1300 block of South Grant Street, where Lipscomb allegedly assaulted a woman before a bystander intervened and helped the victim escape. The second attack occurred Jan. 27 near South Grant Street and East Ellsworth Avenue, when Lipscomb allegedly assaulted a woman who was running; she fought back and screamed until the suspect fled.

Lipscomb was arrested Saturday and is being held on suspicion of second-degree assault, third-degree assault, and second-degree attempted kidnapping. Investigators said physical and forensic evidence gathered from both crime scenes led them to identify Lipscomb as the suspect.

Denver police had issued a public warning on Jan. 30 urging residents in the area to remain vigilant following the two incidents. No motive for the attacks has been released.

stories.headline: Defense Secretary Hegseth visits Colorado space and defense firms, pledges to cut Pentagon red tape for small contractors
stories.summary:
Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth visited Colorado defense and space companies Monday as part of his Arsenal of Freedom Tour, stopping at True Anomaly — a Denver-area firm — and Sierra Space to pledge reforms aimed at giving smaller defense businesses greater access to Pentagon contracts.

Hegseth criticized the department's reliance on a handful of large contractors and long sole-source deals, some of which he said have cost billions over 10 to 15 years before delivering a finished product. 'If we are going to have space dominance and deterrence, we need to unleash every company possible,' he said in remarks filmed at True Anomaly.

Separately, Air Force Secretary Troy Meink delivered a similar message at the Air and Space Forces Association's Warfare Symposium at the Gaylord Rockies Resort and Convention Center in Aurora, outlining new portfolio acquisition executives with broader authority to manage risk and accelerate contracting. The Space Force has hired two such executives and the Air Force five, with plans to expand to nine and 18 respectively.

The visits coincided with the Department of Defense submitting to Congress a spending plan allocating $500 million for space launch infrastructure and $7.2 billion for space-based sensors, as the Space Force prepares for what commanders describe as an era of autonomous, machine-speed competition in space by 2040.

stories.headline: Nuggets' clutch-time struggles mount as Jokic expresses concern after loss to Warriors
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets' struggles in close late-game situations have deepened following a loss to the Golden State Warriors on Sunday, with star center Nikola Jokic publicly voicing concern about the team's inability to execute down the stretch.

Denver has played in 29 clutch games this season — defined as the final five minutes with the score within five points — winning 14, a mark that ranks in the middle of the league. More alarming are the underlying numbers: the team's defensive rating in clutch situations ranks 29th in the NBA, ahead of only the Brooklyn Nets, dragging Denver's net rating to 27th in those situations. The Nuggets' assist-to-turnover ratio in clutch play ranks 26th.

Perhaps the most puzzling data point is that Denver is 8-2 in clutch games without Jokic and 6-13 in those situations with him on the floor. Jokic himself acknowledged the problem without offering an explanation. 'I don't know what it is,' he said. 'But we need to figure it out.'

The Nuggets returned from the All-Star break sitting third in the Western Conference, but repeated late-game collapses — including turnovers that proved costly against Golden State — have raised questions about the team's postseason readiness. Guard Christian Braun said the issue comes down to ball security and toughness in the final minutes.

stories.headline: Elitch Gardens opens applications for 1,500 seasonal positions ahead of April opening
stories.summary:
Elitch Gardens announced Tuesday it has opened applications for approximately 1,500 seasonal positions as the Denver amusement park prepares to launch its season in April, with job fairs scheduled for this weekend at the park's Front Entry Pavilion.

The hiring fairs will take place Saturday from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. and Sunday from noon to 5 p.m. at 2000 Elitch Circle. Applicants must be at least 16 years old and can interview for positions across rides, aquatics, retail, food service, security, and maintenance departments. Starting pay is $19.29 per hour.

Elitch Gardens is set to open April 11 for Season Pass Appreciation Weekend and to the general public on April 18. Employees receive free park admission on days off, along with complimentary tickets for friends and family.

Interested applicants can apply at ElitchGardens.com/Jobs or contact human resources at 303-505-0880.
